entry.title: Re: Share Story Report issue
entry.content:
Hello,

I used to face this issue as well.



The quick and dirty solution which I found out was - Duplicate the report and then try to share the duplicate report>> It works.

This had worked for me every time I had an issue sharing report.

While I also see that I can share it with one user but not able to share it with other user.
Looking a solution for this one. Thanks

Best Regards
Satyam Nalawade

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content: week 3 done

entry.title: Cursor AI in SAP
entry.content:
In the whole SAP Community, I see nothing on Cursor AI (https://medium.com/@warren_eiserman/supercharge-your-sap-cap-development-with-cursor-ai-85479e7e6a41). I wonder if SAP has spoken out about using Cursor AI, if this is recommended or not.

Imo it is not, as it does not come via SAP like Joule. So SAP can't take responsibility on this. But I wonder how the experts from SAP feel about this. Being an AI powered tool, this seems like a fair subject of matter to me.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Joule Studio: Create Joule Skills and Agents Without Coding (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
This is the all-new SAP CodeJam for Joule Studio, a new tool in SAP Build that lets you create extensions to Joule so users can use SAP's digital assistant in their custom workflows.

The CodeJam is intended to introduce you to the Joule Studio tool so you can create your own:

  • Skills, which let you create predefined execution flows, triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot
  • Agents, which lets you define complex scenarios that the agent can execute by iteratively reasoning and interacting with their environment to achieve a goal, using a selected LLM and triggered by a user's prompt inside the Joule chatbot

The session will include a short, simple introduction to the SAP AI tools and some basic concepts needed for working with Joule Studio—such as AI Core, document grounding, embeddings, and what is an LLM?. You will also have the opportunity to build agents directly using Python.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser of either Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge. Other browsers are not supported.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Tuesday, June 23, 2026, from 09:30 - 16:00 local time in Hyderabad, India (IST). The language of the event and the content will be English.

Event Address: Tekskills India Pvt. Ltd, Survey # 1050, Plot # S - 2, 801 , 8th Floor, Manjeera Trinity, Corporate, JNTU Hi-tech City Road, KPHB 3rd Phase, Kukatpally, Hyderabad, Telangana 500072.

Google Maps: https://g.co/kgs/QHsuo1S

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Tekskills India Pvt Ltd. – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Rekha_DR  and @shrinivasan_neelamegam 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: Re: My Purchase Requisition becomes Advance license
entry.content:
Hello @Kerisha123,

we are suddenly facing the same issue. When I add F1639A_TRAN as you described, I can see  that additional apps are required.

However, these no longer seem to be categorized as "Self-Service", so assigning them individually does not help in this case.



The price category was automatically changed on April 19 during the night without any real prior notice. In the SAP Reference Library you can see that there is a planned change for Private Cloud & On Premise. But not for the Public Cloud Version.

Can you tell me if I'm missing something or how to categorize it correctly?

Thank you in advance!

Best regards
Justin

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TAIT What functionality is available in the SAP...
entry.content:
Dear @davidhe79,

The standard SAP Maintenance Assistant app allows technicians to handle parts/components at execution level before confirmation for example by reviewing required parts, issuing additional or unplanned parts and returning unused parts. SAP also documents that final confirmation can include clearing reservations. Based on SAP documented scope this is execution-side part handling, not full maintenance-order component maintenance

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/dfe7048eb27445619838125576edd247/2508/en-US/f8e902da2d6840ce83f2e07b2872f712.pdf

Summary:

In standard Public Cloud, technicians can adjust part consumption in Maintenance Assistant before confirmation, including adding unplanned parts and returning unused parts. Broader maintenance-order component maintenance is handled outside the mobile technician app

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hello Kerisha,

Thanks for your answer! 

For my case, the two sales staffs need to do the same activities in system, for example maintain the price, processing orders. The sales organization, sales area are same. The only difference is they are responsible for different products.

Based on the requirements, is there any easy way to do the set up? 

I try to only set "value help" and maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup. Pls check the screenshot as below.

But it's not works, I still can check sales orders that order material isn't maintained the authorization group in product master data.

Could you support the following questions?

1. the authorization group in product master data should be maintained directly with any value or text, is there any other steps for the creation of authorization group?

2.If we only restrict by products level, all other activities are same, can I only maintain "value help" restricted? or "Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help", and "Value Help" all level need to be maintained?

3. Can I only maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup, ignore all other setting? like sales area, sales org info.

Thank you!

Best regards,

Joey

entry.title: SAP CALM Health Monitoring
entry.content:
Hi ,

 

We are connecting our sap managed system to sap cloud alm for setting up health monitoring . Managed system is SAP Business Suite 7. We are facing issue in network pre-requisite and we are not using cloud connector . what urls /ports/ips should be whitelisted in the network ? If you tell us the list as our process for firewall opening is very exhaustive and want to close the request at one go .

Regards,

Nidhi

entry.title: Re: I copied a standard form with SBF transactioin, but t...
entry.content:
Hello @SAP-Slzr_1 


You copied only the Adobe Form , not the form interface (data source) .

Because of this, the copied form is not bound to the transaction data , so only static text prints.

Fix:

Copy the interface (SFP / form data interface) as well.
Assign the new interface to your new Z form .
Activate both interface and form .
Test again — transaction data will appear.

In short:
Form + Interface must be copied and linked together, not just the form.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Logistics Invoice Verification in SAP MM: End‑to‑End Guide with Practical Insights
entry.content: Thank you for the information .

entry.title: Re: Project Setup Agent | SAP Business AI | Agentic AI | EPPM

entry.title: Re: Automate Job Requisition Updates from Position Data Using Integration Center
entry.content: I'm hoping that it doesn't or wouldn't auto update HMs on requisitions, since those were conveyed to us as momentary snapshots upon creation (regardless of subsequent HM change/adjustment/RBP removal), short of manual update. We have reqs often set up with HM delegates or level up HMs covering for mult business groups within a certain org.

entry.title: Issue with POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT, need to select iban

Hi,

We are using POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT to post documents and we are facing a problem when the BP has many IBANs, no one is selected as default and I get an error 

Is it possible to select one of them? Also, is it possible to pass a custom iban to POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT?

Thanks! 

maría

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2025 FSP01: job "/IWFND/METERING_AGG" is failing
entry.content:
Implement SAP note 3741259  , It has been updated 17 April 26

Prerequisites - Please implement note 3646646 and 3691927  before applying this note.

Best regards,

Ben

entry.title: CX Onboarding: Organizational Change Management as the Key to a Successful CX Implementation
entry.content:
Organizational Change Management as the Key to a Successful CX Implementation

Customer Experience (CX) implementations create powerful opportunities to transform how organizations engage with their customers and empower their employees. When employees understand the purpose of the change, feel supported throughout the journey, and are equipped to work in new ways, CX initiatives become a catalyst for sustainable business impact.

In my previous blog, How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices, we briefly touched on Organizational Change Management (OCM) as a fundamental element in mitigating risk during CX implementations. In this article, we’ll take a closer look at what OCM really means in the context of CX, why it must be embedded from day one, and how clear roles and governance help ensure sustainable adoption.

What Is Organizational Change Management?

Cloud transformations fundamentally change how organizations interact with customers and, as a result, how employees work. New cloud systems influence daily routines, introduce new processes, redefine roles, and demand new skills, mindsets, and behaviours.

Organizational Change Management (OCM) ensures that people are prepared, supported, and enabled throughout this transformation. It goes far beyond communication and training. Effective OCM focuses on driving sustained behaviour change, helping organizations move past system setup and go-live toward long-term value realization and improved customer outcomes.

👉Check this blog to become familiar with OCM’s pillars: Launch Your Project: The Broader Perspective Digital Transformation.

Enabling OCM from the Start of the Project

In successful CX projects, OCM is not treated as an “add-on” or a last-minute activity. Instead, it is embedded into the CX initiative from the very beginning, starting in the Plan Your Implementation phase, to ensure alignment between strategy, technology, and people.

CX transformations are business transformations enabled by technology. They typically impact:

  • Sales, service, and marketing teams simultaneously
  • Cross-functional processes spanning multiple systems
  • Customer-facing behaviours and decision-making
  • Organizational culture, ownership, and accountability

When OCM is integrated into CX initiatives, organizations are better positioned to:

  • Drive faster and more sustainable adoption
  • Reduce resistance and change fatigue
  • Increase employee confidence and readiness
  • Deliver consistent, customer-centric experiences

Who Should Be Responsible for OCM in Your Project?

While it is essential to enable change management from the start, it is equally important—and a core pillar of the SAP OCM framework—to establish a clear governance model with well-defined roles and responsibilities. This includes having active executive sponsorship to lend credibility and reinforce the strategic importance of the project across the organization.

OCM is not owned by a single individual or team. Instead, it is a shared responsibility, supported by structured roles that may include:

  • Change Manager
    Owns the change strategy, plans and coordinates OCM activities, and ensures alignment with the overall CX program.
  • Project Manager
    Ensures effective integration between delivery and OCM activities, aligning milestones, communications, and training with the project delivery phases.
  • People Managers
    Play a critical role in reinforcing change locally, addressing concerns, and supporting their teams through the transition.
  • Change Network / Champions
    Act as ambassadors for the change, providing feedback from the frontline and supporting adoption within their areas.

Clear accountability across these roles helps ensure that change is actively managed and that adoption continues well beyond go-live.

👉Explore more about this topic with this learning: Managing the People Side of SAP Cloud Projects.

How CX Onboarding Supports Customers with OCM

From the CX Onboarding perspective, we recognize how critical it is to establish effective change management early in the project lifecycle. To support our customers, we have compiled proven best practices into a simple, structured guide: CX Onboarding Guide for Organizational Change Management.

This content is designed to enable and support customer-led OCM efforts, not to replace them. It helps customers:

  • Understand what Organizational Change Management is
  • See how OCM aligns with the SAP Activate Methodology
  • Use a dedicated roadmap viewer with ready-to-use templates
  • Access learning and enablement programs to upskill change teams

We strongly encourage you to explore these resources and connect with us if you have questions. Our role is to provide guidance, tools, and insights so you can confidently embed OCM into your CX program.

Ultimately, the responsibility for ensuring that change management is a core component of the project, from initiation through post go-live, remains with the customer.

When OCM is treated as a strategic priority rather than a checkbox, CX implementations are far more likely to deliver lasting business and customer value.

 

Thank you for reading this blog. We hope you found the content insightful. Please give us a like or leave a comment; we’d love to hear from you!

💻Want to get in touch with us? Send an email to SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

📞You can also request a one‑on‑one call with one of our onboarding advisors using this form.

👉Key Resources

  • Onboarding Resource Center
  • CX Onboarding Guide for Organizational Change Management.
  • SAP Learning: Managing the People Side of SAP Cloud Projects
  • Community: Launch Your Project: The Broader Perspective Digital Transformation
  • Community: How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices

entry.title: Use of Custom CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE Not Permitted in Development System
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to use a custom business object created via the Custom Business Object and Custom CDS View Fiori apps. A table has been generated, and I am attempting to use the corresponding CDS entity YY1_PURCHASE in my development (RAP) scenario.

However, I am getting the error:
“The use of CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE is not permitted.”

Is it possible to use such custom CDS entities created via key user tools in RAP development? Or is there any restriction in S/4HANA Public Cloud?

Please let me know if this is supported or not.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors AI - AI-Assisted Performance Insights for Manager (Set up and hints)

The option to add the Insights should be available now (with the H1 2026 release) if manager is involved in iterative or collaborative steps.

entry.title: Re: Balance carryforward runs must be performed in sequen...
entry.content:
Hello @DL25 ,

What i understood from screenprints, Your system status is inconsistent between FI and Asset Accounting

-FI carryforward done only till 2023
-You are trying to jump to 2025
-Asset Accounting is already in 2026

 

Error / SituationExplanation & Fix
FINS_BCF_EN135 – Carryforward must be in sequenceYou are trying to run carryforward for 2025, but system has only completed up to 2023. SAP requires year-by-year processing.
FINS_BCF_EN041 – Run for consecutive yearsYou skipped 2024. System does not allow skipping years. You must first complete 2023 → 2024, then continue.
AA708 – Fiscal year change requested for pastAsset Accounting is already in 2026, while FI is behind. This is only a warning, not a blocking error.
Root CauseFI balance carryforward is incomplete, while Asset Accounting has already moved ahead.
Correct SequenceYou must run carryforward in order: ✔ 2023 → 2024 ✔ 2024 → 2025 ✔ 2025 → 2026 (if required)
What NOT to do❌ Do not skip years ❌ Do not try to go backwards ❌ Do not change AA fiscal year
Recommended ActionRun FAGLGVTR (or Fiori app) sequentially for missing years.
Important NoteCarryforward can be safely re-run multiple times without data issues.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Decommissioning Procedure & FAQ
entry.content:
Please can someone clarify what happens to the Onboarding 1.0 dropdown option in Manage Pending Recruits > Select Pending Recruits after 15th May?

We still see it there in preview but unclear if that's because we haven't reached 15th May yet.

We are keen to know this as we could have future dated hires who came through 1.0 sat in manage pending recruits waiting to be hired and need to know if we must complete the hiring before the 1H release hits production or if they can still be hired from manage pending recruits after that date.

Thanks

entry.title: Adding Legacy Main Asset Number to Asset History Sheet (Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi Experts! I would like to ask, if it is possible to add the Legacy Main Asset Number field into the standard report (C_ASSETHISTORYSHEET) or the standard CDS view (I_AssetHistorySheetCube).

We are working on an SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system, so we need a solution that allows us to enhance the standard application without accessing the backend or using traditional ABAP development. We specifically need this field to be visible and functional within the standard Asset History Sheet app.

Could you please advise on how to resolve this? I would greatly appreciate any guidance, suggestions, or alternative approaches.

Thank you very much for your help!

Best regards, Daniel Fedic

entry.title: Re: A New System-Based ABAP Certification Is Here - What Developers Need to Know
entry.content:
Hi, I enrolled for S4D400 practice system, I didn't get service key

I got this only

Please help me to practice in BTP

entry.title: How are others using curricula effectively in the current learner experience?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

While the old homepage definitely had its issues, it worked better for our use of curricula than the current learner experience. We are finding that the new homepage makes it much harder for learners to use curricula the way we intended. Since the system has been moving in this direction for years and I have not seen much discussion about it, I wanted to ask if maybe I am missing something.

Our goal is for learners to complete a curriculum as one package, but the system, especially with the new homepage and other recent changes, seems to steer them toward launching individual items instead.

Two main issues we are seeing:

1) On the current homepage, individual items from their curricula are front and center under Required Learning, and there is no good way to force learners into the curriculum itself like we could on the old homepage. This often leads them to complete items out of order and/or to miss one or more items in a curriculum. For example, we do not want new learners just working through 100 separate items from the Required Learning section on their homepage - we would like them to go into the curriculum to complete all required curriculum items as a cohesive package.

2) Even if a learner opens the curriculum details page, after completing one item the completion confirmation pop-up takes them out of the flow. There is no easy way to return directly to the curriculum and move on to the next item. Instead, they are sent back to the homepage after each completion and have to manually navigate back into the same curriculum again. It has been like this for years - I believe that item completion pop-up was added in 2022 - and still nothing from support/influence to fix this; they say it is working as designed, but there is a breadcrumb link to return to the item's curriculum - you are just unable to press it after completing an item due to the completion pop-up.

Because of this, curricula do not seem very well supported from the learner perspective.

How are others handling this? Are you still using curricula as a learner-facing package, or have you had to adapt your approach?

entry.title: Re: YCOA Account Groups - Verification of Usage and Diffe...
entry.content:
Hello @byd ,

As per my understanding and experience:-

 

TopicABSTRECN
PurposeStandard reconciliation accountSpecial reconciliation account
Direct posting allowed Not allowed Allowed
Integration with subledger Mandatory Optional/Extended
Typical useNormal AR/AP postingsSpecial scenarios (adjustments, alternative recon)
RiskSafe, controlledMore flexible but risky if misused

Use ABST (Standard Case – 95% scenarios)

-Normal customer/vendor postings
-Standard AR/AP integration
-No manual FI postings required


Use RECN when you need:

-Direct FI postings on reconciliation accounts
-Alternative Reconciliation Accounts functionality
-Adjustments without subledger document
-Migration / correction scenarios


If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Unlock the Potential of Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP) in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hello, do we have any update regarding this one point....and if it is still restrictions what would be the workaround 

Data Integration:

CBP is supported by Real Time Integration. Out of the box, an end-to-end process is supported for S4HANA Private Cloud backend with Demand and Supply Segmentation. 

Please note: Real Time Integration is not supported for S4 HANA Public cloud. This restriction also applies to CHaracteristics Based Planning.

entry.title: Deep Dive: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland
entry.content:
Deep Dive: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland

Die Ankündigung die Gehaltsabrechnung für den deutschen Öffentlichen Dienst (ÖD) in den Lösungsumfang der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll aufzunehmen, hat eine sehr positive Resonanz bei ÖD Kunden und die DSAG hervorgerufen. Nun ist es höchste Zeit konkreter zu werden!

In diesem Blog möchten wir folgende Themen erläutern:

  1.             Was beinhaltet die Lösungsumfangserweiterung von SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP)
  2.             Ausblick für das Jahr 2026 und folgende

Was ist für ÖD Kunden eigentlich drin?

Hier empfehlen wir einen Blick auf die Länderspezifika für SF Employee Central Payroll - Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland  zu werfen.

Zusammengefasst: der Lösungsumfang der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) enthält ab 01. Januar 2026 alle Standardfunktionalitäten für die Personalabrechnung Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland (PY-DE-PS). Die Fakten:

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll basiert auf dem SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA Stack
  • Die bekannten ÖD spezifischen Infotypen wie zB. IT0051 VBL/ZVE-Daten,  IT0509 Höherwertige Tätigkeiten,  IT0320 Dienstwohnung  IT0271 Statistiken des öffentlichen Diensts etc. sind wie gewohnt verfügbar
  • Pro Lohnart die im IT0014 Wiederkehrende Be-/Abzüge, IT0015 Ergänzende Zahlung oder IT2010 Entgeltbelege verarbeitet wird, wird definiert ob sie führend in  SF Employee Central oder in SF Employee Central Payroll gepflegt wird
  • Tarife wie zB. TVöD Bund, TVöD VKA, TVöD Pflege, TV-L etc. und ebenso die Beamtenbesoldung werden wie gewohnt abgebildet
  • ÖD spezifische Funktionalitäten wie zB. die automatische Berechnung der Urlaubs- und Krankenlohnaufschläge für unständige Bezüge, die Krankengeldzuschussberechnung, die zeitversetzte Verarbeitung der unständigen Bezüge oder die Altersteilzeit sind selbstverständlich im Standard vorhanden
  • Deutschland und ÖD spezifische Folgeaktivitäten sind im selben Umfang wie in SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA bzw. bisher in SAP ERP HCM vorhanden
  • Das PY Schema D100 für die Abrechnung ist Teil der Auslieferung
  • Kundeneigenes Schemen und Zyklen können nach SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll übernommen werden
  • Customizing Aktivitäten und kundeneigene ABAP Programmierung z.B. über BadIs oder Z-Programme stehen zur Verfügung. Siehe Hierzu empfehlen wir die Dokumentation im SAP Help Portal zum Thema Modifikation und Erweiterbarkeit von SF Employee Central Payroll. 

 

Rahmenbedingungen für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland

Die wichtigste Botschaft: Der Scope der SF Employee Central Payroll  unterscheidet sich hinsichtlich der Personalabrechnung für den Öffentlichen Dienst Deutschland inklusive Folgeaktivitäten, Auswertungen und Statistiken nicht von SAP S/4HANA. 

Natürlich gilt der allgemeine Umfang der Lösung bzw. die Rahmenbedingungen gemäß der Produktbeschreibung und der Funktionsumfangsbeschreibung für SF Employee Central Payroll auch für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland. SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll ist der Teil der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite der für die Gehaltsabrechnung zuständig ist. Teile des HCM-Funktionsumfangs, die nicht direkt zur Gehaltsabrechnung gehören, sind wegen des starken Fokus auf Gehaltsabrechnung nicht im Lösungsumfang der EC Payroll verfügbar. Dazu gehören z.B. das Reisekostenmanagement, das Cross Application Time Sheet (CATS), die Learning Solution (LSO) oder eine Personaleinsatzplanung.

Dies vorausgeschickt möchten wir auf einige für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland wichtige Eigenheiten hinweisen. Zur besseren Lesbarkeit hier nur die zusätzlich für ÖD Kunden besonders relevanten Klarstellungen und Einschränkungen.

Kundenspezifische LösungVerwendung in Employee Central Payroll nicht erlaubt
Felder Dienstart/Unterdienstart in IT0001: Grundlegende Erweiterbarkeit: Zuordnung von Employee-Central-Feldern zu InfotypenVersorgungsadministration (VADM)
ZfA-Kommunikation über den SAP Standard hinausNachversicherungsadministration (NV)
 Organisationsmanagement Öffentliche Verwaltungen (PA-OM, PA-PM-OM)
 Stellenwirtschaft inklusive Obligoprozessor (PA-PM-CP) und Personal Budget Plan Management (PA-PM-BM)
 Reisekostenmanagement (FI-TV)
 Personaleinsatzplanung

Quelle: Länderspezifika für SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll - Öffentlicher Dienst Deutschland    Stand März 2026

Kunden aus dem Kern der öffentlichen Verwaltung z.B. aus Bund, Ländern und Kommunen, die VADM für die Versorgung Ihrer Beamten benötigen oder gesetzliche Anforderungen mit der Stellenwirtschaft befriedigen müssen, können dies nicht in der SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (Stand März 2026). Weiteres dazu im Kapitel Ausblick.

Empfehlung für den Umgang mit Basisbezügen für den ÖD

Aufgrund der Verknüpfungen zu verschiedenen anderen ÖD-spezifischen Infotypen wird bis auf weiteres empfohlen, Basisbezüge (Infotyp 0008) im öffentlichen Dienst direkt in Employee Central Payroll zu verwalten und nicht aus dem Compensation Portlet in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central zu replizieren.

Dementsprechend sollten Basisbezüge (Infotyp 0008) von der Point-to-Point Replikation im Customizing (Transaktion SPRO) ausgeschlossen werden, indem Sie die beschriebenen Schritte aus der ECP Dokumentation vornehmen.

 

Voraussetzungen für SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

Kunden die bestehendes Customizing und / oder kundeneigenen Code aus einem bestehenden SAP HCM System übernehmen möchten, können dies tun solange Sie:

  1. Funktionsumfangsbeschreibung, Produktbeschreibung und dabei insbesondere Modifikation und Erweiterbarkeit von SAP SF Employee Central Payroll beachten
  2. Sicherstellen, dass sowohl Customizing als auch kundeneigener Code mit SAP S/4HANA kompatibel sind. Beachten sie dabei insbesondere die SAP Note 3091160 - SAP HCM für SAP S/4HANA – Allgemeiner Ansatz und Strategie, welche Aufschluss über HCM Simplification Items in S/4HANA gibt

Als Startpunkt zur Identifikation von Simplification Items bietet sich der SAP Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors HCM an. 

 

Ausblick für das Jahr 2026 und folgende

Zusammen mit unseren Kunden begeben wir uns auf die Reise dem Öffentliche Dienst in Deutschland mit der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite moderne HCM Prozesse digital in der Cloud zur Verfügung zu stellen, ohne dabei Abstriche in Sachen Sicherheit, Souveränität und Funktionsumfang zu machen. 

SAP ist bewusst, dass besonders Kunden aus dem Kern der öffentlichen Verwaltung auf Funktionalitäten wie Versorgungsadministration oder Stellenbewirtschaftung nicht verzichten können. Zusammen mit unseren Partnern und der DSAG arbeiten wir an diesen und weiteren Themen um letztlich mit allen Kunden aus dem Öffentlichen Dienst das Ziel zu erreichen.

Es gilt der übliche Disclaimer: Dies ist natürlich keine Zusage bestimmte Funktionalitäten zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt oder überhaupt zu liefern. Die Roadmap wird sobald konkret absehbar über die dafür vorgesehen Kanäle wie Roadmap Explorer oder What's New Viewer kommuniziert.

HR souverän und sicher - SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite in der SAP Sovereign Cloud

Die Sicherheitsmaßnahmen beim Betrieb von SAP Cloud Lösungen entsprechen den höchsten Standards für Cybersicherheit, Betrieb und Datenschutz. Dies gilt insbesondere für kritische Persönliche Mitarbeiter- und Organisationsdaten in der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite. Ausführlich informiert hier die Webseite SAP Trust Center - Sicherheit.

Zusätzliche SAP-Sovereign-Cloud-Angebote, sind eigenständige Angebote unseres Cloud-Portfolios, die auf die anspruchsvollsten Anforderungen in allen vier Dimensionen der digitalen Souveränität zugeschnitten sind: Daten, Betrieb, Recht und Technik.
Damit werden die strengeren regulatorischen und sicherheitstechnischen Anforderungen bestimmter Branchen, wie in teilen des Öffentlichen Dienstes, gefordert erfüllt.

Besonders relevant für unsere ÖD Kunden in Deutschland ist das in Deutschland gehostete Angebot SAP Cloud Infrastructure. Die Verfügbarkeit der SAP SuccessFactors HCM Suite inklusive Payroll ist dort bis Ende 2026 geplant.

Weitere Informationen finden Sie unter SAP Sovereign Cloud auf www.sap.com.

 

Fazit

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll basierend auf SAP S/4HANA  und tief integriert mit SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  ist die moderne Payroll für den Öffentlichen Dienst in Deutschland. Dabei bietet sie aus Perspektive der Gehaltsabrechnung den vollen von SAP gewohnten Leistungsumfang in einem schlanken Kosteneffizienten Cloud Deployment. SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll vereint Effektivität, Effizienz und Sicherheit/Souveränität mit einer starken AI basierten Roadmap.

SAP arbeitet zusammen mit Partnern daran jedem Kunden den Umstieg auf die Full Cloud mit SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll zu ermöglichen.

Für Informationen zur SAP HCM Strategie, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, Readiness Check for SAP SuccessFactors und zu weiteren Themen, empfehlen wir einen Besuch unserer HCM Move Seite.

entry.title: Hello Everyone, Excited to Join This Forum!
entry.content: Hi everyone! I’m new to this forum and really excited to be part of this community. I’m here to learn, share ideas, and connect with others. I hope to gain valuable knowledge and contribute positively. Looking forward to engaging discussions and meeting amazing people here!

entry.title: Re: Failed to enroll - system-based SAP Certification

Has anyone encountered this? Anyone with data persistence knowledge can share anything more about it? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: sap business one b1 plant maintenance export
entry.content: In SAP Business One you can export Work Orders and Diagnostic Sheet data to Excel directly. Open the Work Order list in the Plant Maintenance module, then go to Data and click Export to Excel to download the data. For Diagnostic Sheets, open the same screen and use the same export option. If you need more specific data, use Query Generator or Query Manager to fetch the required fields and then export the results to Excel.

entry.title: SAP DM PPD - how to reset/empty string array
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are having some local arrays in our PPDs now once the loop complete, we have to empty the local array for the next iteration. but now the problem is we only have the add array/remove array item in PCo functions.

can anyone please help us if there are items in the array then how we can completely empty array in SAP DM.

Quick help will be appreciated.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?

entry.title: Re: Restrict Plant in MB51 Report
entry.content: You can't enhance Selection Screen Events (see the possible positions: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_758_index_htm/7.58/en-US/index.htm?file=abenimplicit_enh_points.htm) but you may implement the Enhancement Point RM07DOCS_01 (Spot ES_RM07DOCS).

entry.title: Entering a long text while creating a dowpayment via FIORI app id F1688
entry.content:
Dear experts, 

I am creating a downpayment via FIORI app id F1688 - Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests. 

And as we have payment details with the text more than 170 symbols the field BSEG-SGTXT doesn't suit our requirement. 

For example, at Create Supplier Down Payment Requests entering a long text is available via Extras ---> Text (please find the screenshot below). 

That would be great if there is any chance to add Extras ---> Text somehow to that fiori app. (F1688)

Maybe we should activate something or take some steps? If it is not possible I would appreciate to get any tips or advices from you.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: MIRO Header Enhancement with custom fields
entry.content: Try BAdI first, because its technology is newer than Customer Exit (CMOD). Also, always refer to SAP official documentation before deciding (SAP Help Portal and SAP Notes).

entry.title: Re: SAP DRC Multiple Cloud Regions
entry.content: If you are using electronic invoices for France, yes according to documentation you need cf-eu02

entry.title: How to Debug the Event Consumption class in SAP BTP ABAP system for an event in Event Mesh ?
entry.content:
Hi All , 

How can i debug the event consumption class in ADT ??

Basically i am trying to consume an event in SAP BTP ABAP system . i have done the necessary objects following this Event Consumption in SAP BTP ABAP   .

1-> Event Consumption Model in SAP BTP ABAP system ( via ADT ) 
2-> Communication Arrangement , Scenario  .

for ex -My IB Communication user is CC00000012 (EVENT_CONSUMER) linked to communication systems for IB services for the Comm arrangement of EventMesh to SAP BTP system based on the custom Communication Scenario . ( Step 9) .

While testing  , i see the event is consumed successfully , however i dont see this Communication user available in F4 help for User External Debugger settings in ADT . I found in ADT ,the F4 help is limited to Dialog user . 

 

Is there any way to debug the event consumption class ?

Thank You .

entry.title: neuer Bewertungsbereich für Versicherungswerte
entry.content:
Guten Tag, können Sie mir bitte helfen zu verstehen, wie der neue Bewertungsbereich für Versicherungswerte angelegt werden muss?

Ich habe jetzt mit allen hier zur Verfügung stehenden Informationen einen Bewertungsbereich erstellt. Dieser bringt den Fehler E006 obwohl ich einen Anlage ohne Wert habe.

Zudem ist mir unklar, wie ich die Versicherungswerte dann von 'migriere' in den neuen Bereich hinein?

Ebenfalls würde ich gerne den Index je Anlagenklasse vorgeben aber das Feld in Transaktion OAYZ ist ausgegraut. 

Gibt es irgendwo eine vernünftige Anleitung?

Vielen Dank im Voraus

S. Horsch

entry.title: Re: Which ledger scenarios are available as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2302?
entry.content:
Hi Verena,

Regarding the "local accounting standard", are you referring to the local accounting standard in other countries? If we are planning to use the parallel ledger and suggest changing the default 0L to the corporate accounting standard?

Also, could you please clarify which corporate accounting standard you are referring to? which specific accounting standard will be applied?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Use of Custom CDS Entity YY1_PURCHASE Not Permitted i...
entry.content:
Dear @S_DEV,

No in Public Cloud this should be treated as a standard restriction. YY1_PURCHASE created via Custom Business Object or Custom CDS View belongs to the key user extensibility layer, while RAP in ADT is part of developer extensibility. SAP states that repository objects created with ABAP for Key Users can’t be released for developer extensibility so they cannot be used as direct RAP dependencies

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/533228e1e854433ab16d013f161ca509.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-cloud-background-concepts-and-overview/cloud-optimized-abap-language

https://help.sap.com/doc/112b8531132e43398ed50b2ad38ab14b/latest/en-US/ffa919727c624f25a95bd2f9443244f4.pdf

The supported approach is to either build the RAP model on released developer objects or expose the custom business object through its generated OData service and consume it through a Custom Communication Scenario / API instead of referencing the YY1_* CDS entity directly. SAP documents service generation for Custom Business Objects and the use of the generated YY1_*_CDS service in a custom communication scenario

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/862bb7b0024145db8a55acb7df3684f6/SHIP/en-US/Develop_a_Custom_UI_on_SAP_Cloud_Platform_and_Deploy_it_to_SAP_S4HANA_Cloud.pdf

Summary:

Direct use of YY1_PURCHASE in RAP is not supported in Public Cloud. The supported option is service-based consumption of the key-user object or a RAP model built on released developer objects

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Launch ByDesign UIs from External Applications

entry.title: Re: My Purchase Requisition becomes Advance license
entry.content:
Hello @Kerisha123  & @SenthilkumaranSap 

I have now assigned the app directly again as described.
At the moment, this seems to work, even though we removed the apps that are supposedly dependent (advanced price category). It still works. Are these apps not actually required?

Nevertheless, the change surprised me, as according to the Reference Library only the Private Cloud and On-Premise versions were supposed to be affected by this switch to “Advanced” in Q2.
I also would not have expected this as part of a maintenance update.

Is there a way to track such changes in advance? Or was this not intended in this case?

Best regards
Justin

entry.title: Batch number creation should not dictate EWM distribution
entry.content:
Hello team,

 

I have created two ASN.

180000050 – Batch number I did not assign during ASN creation

180000049 – Batch number I have assigned during ASN creation

 

But Batch number creation should not dictate which IBD will be distributed or which IBD should not be disturbed in WM public cloud

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Your User ID: GE307785

Week 1

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud enables user-based authoriza...
entry.content:
Hi @Joey3

in this case, I would work with leading and derived roles:

  • Create one leading role which contains all business catalogs and apps that are needed.
  • Then you can create two derived business roles from the leading role.
  • In each derived business role, you can set the restrictions as you need them per role.

See: How to Create Leading and Derived Business Roles | SAP Help Portal

 

Whether you can see the data is controlled by the definition in “Read, Value Help”.

“I try to only set "value help" and maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup. Pls check the screenshot as below. But it's not works, I still can check sales orders that order material isn't maintained the authorization group in product master data.”

→ This is not sufficient, you need to maintain the field Authorization Group as “Read, Value Help”. However, I would suggest to also maintain "Write, Read, Value Help" (see explanation below).

To do so, you need to change the overall Read Access of the role from “Unrestricted” to “Restricted”, then the field definitions for “Read, Value Help” will become visible.

 

 

Answers to each question:

 

1) The authorization group in product master data should be maintained directly with any value or text, is there any other steps for the creation of authorization group?

No, there are no preceding steps.

Once you maintain a value in the field “authorization group” in product master data:

This value automatically becomes available for selection as a restriction value:

 

2) If we only restrict by products level, all other activities are same, can I only maintain "value help" restricted? or "Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help", and "Value Help" all level need to be maintained?

As described above, it is necessary to maintain "Read, Value Help" to define which values can be seen. However, if you set your role’s Write Access to “Unrestricted”, this will override a more specific read permission. Therefore, I would advise you to maintain all three ("Write, Read, Value Help", "Read, Value Help", and "Value Help" in each role).

 

3) Can I only maintain Authorization Group for Product in "General" setup, ignore all other setting? like sales area, sales org info.

Yes, this is an either / or situation. You can work with Authorization Group for Product or Authorization Group for Product Group or Authorization Group for Product Type, whichever works best for your specific requirement.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: SAP Learning Management System- Item Completion Assignment Profile Question
entry.content:
For our Leadership Training program, our team has multiple items that all constitute substitute credit for each other.
We want the curricula & item to fall off their profile after they receive credit for any of the other items so in the assignment profile we have the rule below.

Has anyone found a way to select multiple items and add them all at once to this rule? We have 17 items to add to this rule in every single one of the assignment profiles we have built. If there is not a way to select multiple items, has anyone figured out a way to copy and paste the values from one assignment profile rule to the next for this rule? It is not letting me copy and paste either.

 

Appreciate the help!

entry.title: Change icon in performance review rating scale
entry.content: In performance reviews, is it possible to change the icon associated with the rating scale? Currently, stars are displayed, but I'd like to know if there are other options available.

@KevinR  @noravonthenen 

Week 3 Challenge completed

User ID - AC300864U12

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing in Datasphere?
entry.content: there is no AUDIT_LOG table in the schema DWC_AUDIT_READER. It is DPP_AUDIT_LOG table and it contains audit log for database operations, SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE and DELTE. But doesn't have the information of user activity.

entry.title: Email Template not generating in Q-System after Software Collection Import
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue with a custom Email Template in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I have created a custom template in our Development environment and moved it to the Quality system using the Export/Import Software Collections apps. While the template is visible in the "Maintain Email Templates" app in the Q-system, the emails are no longer generating for my business documents.

Could anyone point me toward which specific configuration or mapping is most commonly missed when transporting these objects via Software Collections? Is there a specific activation step required in the Q-system after the import?

Thanks in advance for your help.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing...

entry.content:
It is a really great and helpful tool. I use it with Microsoft Edge in a german localization.
I recognized an issue: No matter what I enter in the Fiori App searchbar, the result is always empty.

entry.title: Re: Sizing the SAP Datasphere Object Store
entry.content:
Following the comment from Kristian Appel, can one object store perform with an scenario of Sales orders with 7.5 billion records per month, assuming that that vloumne should include Company code as partition criteria and having 70 company codes for example.  What is the SAP best practise architecture on the new technology with this level of records ?
Kind regards,
Mariano

entry.title: Re: How to change message status from RETRY to FAILED whe...
entry.content:
HI Himani,

Yes — fundamentally,If you use HTTP(S) such that CPI initiates the call, then:

CPI owns:
Execution
Error handling
Retry
FAILED vs COMPLETED status

You can implement Custom retry count or Explicit FAILED statuses

But: Event Mesh does not natively expose queues via HTTP pull the way AMQP does
So practically you cannot “read” an Event Mesh queue via HTTP instead of AMQP

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patch 8 to SP5 patch 4 failed !
entry.content: Andrea, I also had issues helping a customer upgrade to SP5 Patch 4. Some of it was self-inflicted, like not disabling CrowdStrike first. However, we also learned about a clustering issue (SAP KB 3724948), so we opted to wait for SP5 Patch 5, which also resolved CVE identified in the April 2026 Security Note.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Veera Venkata Satyanarayana, an Aspiring Mechanical Engineer Stu
entry.content:
Thank you so much for your kind and encouraging words, @StephanieMarley . It truly means a lot to me. I’m still at the beginning of my learning journey, and support like yours motivates me to keep growing.

Yes, I’ve started exploring SAP Learning resources and I’m very interested in learning more about Joule and SAP Build.

Thank you once again for your support and inspiration.

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patch 8 to SP5 patch 4 failed !
entry.content: In any case, the SAP Community would love to hear how your story turned out.

entry.title: Parallel landscape using the separate client
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

we are planning to setup and parallel landscape for project line. usually companies use separate system for parallel project line.

can we use new client in same development system for project purpose.  below in the diagram is the approach I am thinking of.

due to budget constrain we can not have more systems. 

what are the risks of cross client object conflicts.  we are on S/4 hana 2023 private cloud edition (RISE).

are there other risks and limitation also?

Your inputs are appreciated.

Regards

entry.title: Re: MIRO Header Enhancement with custom fields
entry.content:
Look first a some OSS notes/KBA such as 1156325 - BAdIs in the Logistics Invoice Verification environment.

  • But I'm not aware of standard MM BAdI (or Exit) to add such customer data to header (only for item with MRM_ITEM_CUSTFIELDS)
  • You could look to FI BaDI such as BADI_FDCB_SUBBAS** (look a not yet implemented BAdI, depending on your exact requirements you may be required to insure some data exchange between MM and FI BadI implementations)

entry.title: Re: MuleSoft vs SAP Integration Suite: Which One Fits Your Enterprise?
entry.content: Thank you Jarlei. Appreciate the feedback! I will consider covering them in a follow-up post in future.

entry.title: Re: Maintaining serial numbers during Returns order creation
entry.content:
Hi Thiru,

In the Create Customer Return Without Reference app, you can add serial numbers via Menu -> Extras -> Technical Objects.

The OData V4 API for customer returns was enhanced to support serial numbers with release 2602.

I hope this answers your questions.

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Update on SAP HANA Cloud positioning and commercialization in context of SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Partners, we are hosting a focused live session: Update on SAP HANA Cloud positioning and commercialization in context of SAP Business Data Cloud.

In this session, we walk through how SAP HANA Cloud fits inside SAP Business Data Cloud and what this means for your architecture decisions and client conversations.

We will cover three key areas.  First, how SAP HANA Cloud supports scenarios around agentic AI and custom AI-infused applications.  Second, how the commercial model is integrated within SAP Business Data Cloud.  Third, the main technical differences when accessing SAP HANA Cloud via SAP Business Technology Platform versus SAP Business Data Cloud.

We will also look ahead at upcoming changes in the roadmap so you can align your planning with what is coming next.

If you work in data, analytics, or application development in the SAP ecosystem, this session helps you sharpen how you position SAP HANA Cloud with your customers.

https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013682.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPAnalyticsCloud #DataArchitecture #CloudData

entry.title: Re: Issue with POSTING_INTERFACE_DOCUMENT, need to select iban
entry.content:
• To select an IBAN for the BP, ask functionals for some rules (currency, country, etc.)
  • Did  you already try to add a record with FNAM value such as 'BBSEG-IBAN' to T_FTPOST table parameter ?
    (should be available since 696488 - RFBIBL00: Batch input data transfer of IBAN data a long time ago)

entry.title: Re: Maintaining serial numbers during Returns order creation
entry.content: Thanks a lot. This helps a lot in proceeding.

entry.title: Re: SF EC/ECP Data transformation with Integration with S...
entry.content:
#GenAI Assisted Content 

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

The following information has been provided by our internal chatbot:

Migration and Data Transformation Considerations

Migrating from an on-prem ECC system to SAP SuccessFactors EC/ECP and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud involves several considerations, especially regarding data transformation and integration.

1. Retro Calculations Across Two Controlling Areas

SAP does not explicitly support retro payroll calculations that span two different controlling areas without transforming historical records. It is crucial to ensure that historical data aligns with the new controlling area to avoid discrepancies during retro calculations.

2. Data Integrity Within EC and ECP

Maintaining historical data with references to the old controlling area post-migration can lead to data integrity issues. SAP recommends aligning all data with the new controlling area structure to ensure a clean configuration for the new system.

3. Payroll Posting File Reconciliation

Without transforming historical data within EC/ECP, there is a risk of inaccuracies in the payroll posting file. Ensuring data transformation before running payroll in ECP and posting results to S4 is essential for accuracy and completeness.

4. Audit Trail and Compliance

Updating historical records to reflect a new controlling area requires careful consideration of audit trails. SAP advises maintaining detailed documentation of changes to ensure compliance and traceability.

5. SAP’s Recommended Approach

SAP recommends transforming historical data within EC/ECP to align with the new S4 controlling area structure at go-live. This transformation is supported and recommended to ensure data consistency and integrity. Utilizing SAP’s Master Data Integration service can facilitate this process. For more information, see Streamlining the Hire-to-Retire Process with SAP One Domain Model .

Tools and Methodologies

SAP provides tools like the SAP Master Data Integration service to assist in data transformation and integration. It is advisable to consult SAP’s documentation and support for detailed guidance on implementing these changes. For more information, see Integrating Employee Central with S/4HANA Cloud: Getting it running and solving issues - Link Collection.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How do I get Object change user activity for Auditing...
entry.content: Indeed, Security -> Activities should provide you this info.

entry.title: Set employee personnel number ranges in s/4 HANA Public cloud to External
entry.content:
We have a requirement that when a finance vendor payment is being processed, the finance person would like to see the external personnel ID (Workday ID) in the SAP personnel number field on the UI. This would require that the external personnel number range be set to external so that we can populate it with the Workday ID.

How to assign Personnal ID in SAP to external number range

entry.title: Re: Restrict Plant in MB51 Report
entry.content:
Hello- No need to disturb standard MB51 design - you can create a new t.code ZMB51(copying design of MB51) and add your expected design with your 5 plants new logic with error message. Involve ABAP team and GRC team for this requirement on ABAP development along with by having different GRC role.

Regards,

entry.title: AFO Queries with SAP HANA Analytical Privileges
entry.content:
Hi,

Id like to know if the following scenario is possible with SSO. 

I have users logging into AFO to query reports. The reports in question are going to be using SQL Analytical privileges to secure them. The problem I currently have is the technical user SAPHANADB is querying the caluclation view, which means the auth check is being done on the technical user. I would like some sort of principal propagation SSO from AFO, through B4H and into HANA. 

I have checked the DBMS tab in SU01 and both Logon Ticket and Assertion has been ticked. The Kerberos CN is updated in both B4H and HANA. The user also can log into HANA with SSO with Kerberos. 

Is this actually possible with AFO using Analytical Privileges?
